etymology.description: semantic 扌(扌) (shǒu, "hand") + phonetic 童(童) (tóng) -> to knock; to collide
etymology.interpretation: The character 撞(zhuàng, "to knock; to collide") combines the semantic component 扌(扌) (shǒu, "hand") with a phonetic component 童(童) (tóng). The hand indicates action or contact, which is involved in the act of knocking or colliding, while 童(童) provides the pronunciation.

examples.chinese: 他骑车撞到了树(他騎車撞到了樹)。
examples.pinyin: Tā qí chē zhuàng dào le shù.
examples.english: He rode his bike and hit the tree.

examples.chinese: 两辆车在交叉口相撞(兩輛車在交叉口相撞)。
examples.pinyin: Liǎng liàng chē zài jiāochākǒu xiāngzhuàng.
examples.english: Two cars collided at the intersection.

examples.chinese: 那只鸟撞到了窗户(那隻鳥撞到了窗戶)。
examples.pinyin: Nà zhī niǎo zhuàng dào le chuānghù.
